summary:
Baricitinib is an approved JAK inhibitor that has demonstrated significant efficacy in reducing disease activity and improving function in patients with moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis, as confirmed by large-scale randomized controlled trials. Clinical practice guidelines from major rheumatology societies recognize baricitinib as a first-line biologic therapy option due to strong evidence supporting its safety and effectiveness compared to conventional DMARDs.  

Note: This summary focuses only on the most critical clinical facts related to baricitinib’s use in rheumatoid arthritis without referencing unrelated medications listed elsewhere.
